title: Deep phenotyping of the unselected COPSAC2010 birth cohort study.
ehr_evidence: The study describes a prospective clinical cohort where data is collected during clinic visits and biobanking, rather than from an existing EHR database.
summary: The COPSAC 2010 birth cohort study investigates the interaction between perinatal exposures (microbiome, maternal nutrition) and genetic factors in the development of chronic inflammatory diseases like asthma in children.
key_findings: High adherence rate (95-98%) for clinic visits.
Establishment of a longitudinal framework for deep phenotyping and biobanking.
study_design: Prospective birth cohort study with clinical follow-up and randomized controlled trials
population: Pregnant women and their offspring (children up to age 3) in eastern Denmark
exposure_domain: human microbiome, maternal nutrition (vitamin D, fish oil), perinatal exposures
